name: Ao Qin
description: Ao Qin, one of the Dragon Kings of the Four Seas in Chinese mythology, reigns over the Eastern Sea (Dong Hai). He is depicted as a powerful and majestic dragon, embodying the vastness and untamable nature of the ocean. Ao Qin's primary responsibilities involve controlling the weather, particularly rainfall, and ensuring the prosperity of the coastal regions under his dominion. He resides in a magnificent underwater palace, filled with treasures and guarded by loyal aquatic creatures. While often portrayed as benevolent towards humans who respect the sea, Ao Qin, like other Dragon Kings, can be wrathful when angered, unleashing storms and floods upon those who disrespect his authority or the balance of nature. His influence extends to the lives of fishermen, sailors, and anyone dependent on the sea's bounty, making him a significant figure in traditional Chinese beliefs and folklore. He is often invoked for good fortune and protection during maritime activities and is a symbol of the power and mystery of the ocean depths.
subtitle: Dragon King of the Eastern Sea

keyMyths.title: The Dragon King's Generosity
keyMyths.description: In a tale of benevolence, Ao Qin grants a struggling fisherman a magical pearl that ensures a bountiful catch. The fisherman, initially overjoyed, grows greedy and attempts to steal more from the Dragon King's realm. Angered by this avarice, Ao Qin unleashes a fierce storm, reminding humanity of the sea's power and the importance of respect for its gifts. The story serves as a cautionary fable about greed and the consequences of disrespecting nature's balance.
keyMyths.source: Werner, E. T. C. (1922). *Myths and Legends of China*. George G. Harrap & Co.

keyMyths.title: The Stolen Armor
keyMyths.description: This myth tells of a time when a celestial warrior needed a powerful armor to defeat a monstrous threat. He sought the help of Ao Qin, who possessed a legendary set of scales imbued with the power of the ocean. Ao Qin, hesitant at first, eventually agrees to lend the armor, understanding the gravity of the situation. The warrior successfully defeats the monster, and the armor is returned, solidifying the alliance between the celestial and aquatic realms.

keyMyths.title: Ao Qin and the Drought
keyMyths.description: During a severe drought, the land suffered immensely. The people pleaded with Ao Qin for rain, but he remained unresponsive, bound by celestial rules dictating the timing of rainfall. However, witnessing the suffering of the people, Ao Qin defied the rules and brought forth a life-saving rainstorm. This act of compassion, though technically an act of disobedience, highlights his benevolent nature and his willingness to prioritize the well-being of humanity over strict adherence to divine law. He faced consequences for his actions, showcasing the complex moral landscape of Chinese mythology.
keyMyths.source: Birrell, A. (1999). *Chinese Mythology: An Introduction*. Johns Hopkins University Press.

sources.author: Werner, E. T. C.
